_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma or equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Patient has a history of undergoing vitreoretinal surgery with silicon oil insertion in the same eye
Recurrent retinal detachment (as a complication of silicon oil injection/removal)
Silicone oil in the anterior chamber
Adherence of oil droplets to an intraocular lens
Unexplained visual loss following removal of silicon oil

claim.label: Sticker/Barcode of the Intraocular lens (IOL) (if IOL used)
Intraoperative photograph with patient ID, time and date stamp (optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Patient has a history of undergoing vitreoretinal surgery with Silicon oil insertion in the same eye?
Documentary evidence of presence of silicon oil on fundus examination/ B-scan is provided?
Clinical photo of Affected Eye (R or L) with full face photograph is provided?

Detailed Clinical notes including history (diabetes/trauma), clinical symptoms (visual haze/ floaters/ cloudy vision/ photophobia/ shadow/ curtain effect/ cobwebs) and examination findings (fundus examination) are present?

cpd_claim.q: OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed (including steps indication removal of silicon oil) and the outcomes of surgery?
Documents show appropriate post-op care and follow-up advice?

common_queries: Patient lacks a history of undergoing vitreoretinal surgery with Silicon oil insertion in the same eye.
No documentary evidence (from fundus examination or B-scan) of silicon oil presence provided at pre-authorization.
Clinical notes incomplete regarding detailed history or examination findings (fundus examination).
Affected eye not labelled R or L in the clinical photograph, or full face photograph not provided.
Procedure/operative notes missing at claim submission or lack detail on surgical steps for silicon oil removal and outcomes.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination.
complications_of_silicon_oil_injection_r: Silicone oil in the anterior chamber
Adherence of oil droplets to an intraocular lens
investigations: Fundus examination using direct/ indirect ophthalmoscope
